arts.ttl: Two of Celtic's Champions League opponents get destroyed on matchday one
arts.sum: And whilst the Celtic boss will take nothing for granted against Bratislava, a win would be a huge boost for Rodgers, especially after the hammerings Young Boys and Dinamo Zagreb took last night in their league phase opener. Young Boys opened up their Champions League campaign with a home tie against another of Celtic’s opponents, Aston Villa.

arts.ttl: The skyrocketing numbers behind Celtic's bid to create Champions League history with play-off only the start
arts.sum: The Hoops are huge outsiders to lift the famous trophy - but a spot in the knockouts has been handed a realistic chance

arts.ttl: What happened the last time Celtic met Slovan Bratislava
arts.sum: Rewinding to the 1963/64 campaign, Celtic and Slovan Bratislava were drawn against each other in the European Cup Winners’ Cup quarter-final, which took place over two legs. Nevertheless, Slovan Bratislava boss Vladimir Weiss has challenged his side to claim a shock result despite acknowledging the unique atmosphere the Slovak champions will need to deal with.

arts.ttl: The mind boggling Celtic sums that have Hotline detecting Rangers panic amid escalating Aberdeen fear
arts.sum: Alan Galindo manned the phones as chat over cash dominated with Craig Levein's sacking also a popular topic

arts.ttl: Dean Saunders has his say on what could be difficult for Celtic in Champions League
arts.sum: Celtic’s domestic record and success at that level cannot be argued with but what can be better is how the team take their form from the Scottish domestic competitions to help improve their fortunes in Europe. Saunders told talkSPORT, “Without being over-critical of the Scottish league, do you think part of the reason why they’re not successful in Europe is because they can coast through three games on the bounce and then [struggle] to step up?

arts.ttl: Beaming Kris Boyd trolls Celtic as Rangers hero offers Champions League reminder with brutal mic drop
arts.sum: Brendan Rodgers' side will return to Europe's elite competition when they face Slovan Bratislava

arts.ttl: Kris Boyd Celtic dig falls flat as Sky Sports host quickly corrects Rangers hero

arts.ttl: Celtic vs Slovan Bratislava: TV channel, kick-off time, team news, referee & VAR
arts.sum: If you have a Celtic TV subscription, you can listen to live audio coverage of the Slovan Bratislava game and then watch a full match video replay. Below, 67 Hail Hail has everything you need to know about the Slovan Bratislava game including how to watch on TV, the latest team news, the appointed officials and more.

arts.ttl: Lennon breaks Bucharest silence, Shearer is Celtic believer and 2 Champions League risks named – Parkhead news bulletin
arts.sum: It's a big night in Glasgow's east end tonight as Brendan Rodgers and his troops take to the Champions League stage

arts.ttl: Stephen McGinn left surprised by new signing ahead of Celtic vs Slovan Bratislava, bodes well
arts.sum: Stephen McGinn is in no doubt that Engels will start and how you don’t pay such a huge fee for a player and leave him out of your first Champions League game. “First Champions League night, Celtic Park, I think it’s a huge game for the football club, in terms of everyone’s hopes of progressing.
